content: For vegetarian protein, add dal, chana, rajma, sprouts, paneer, tofu, soy chunks, curd, Greek yogurt, nuts, or seeds depending on the meal.
source: Curated nutrition guidance aligned with balanced-diet principles
safety: Keep advice food-focused and avoid changing prescribed supplements or medicines.

content: For vegan protein, use tofu, tempeh, soy chunks, dal, chana, rajma, sprouts, peanuts, seeds, or hummus with grains for a more complete meal.
source: Curated nutrition guidance aligned with balanced-diet principles
safety: Check allergies and digestive tolerance before increasing legumes or soy.

content: For non-vegetarian or eggetarian meals, eggs, fish, chicken, curd, or lean meat can improve protein adequacy when paired with vegetables and whole grains.
source: Curated nutrition guidance aligned with balanced-diet principles
safety: Use safe food handling and follow clinician advice for cholesterol, kidney disease, or other medical restrictions.

content: For steadier energy, pair complex carbohydrates with protein, fiber-rich vegetables, and fluids instead of having mostly refined carbs or only tea/coffee.
source: Curated nutrition guidance aligned with balanced-diet principles
safety: Persistent fatigue should be discussed with a qualified clinician.

content: For muscle gain, distribute protein across meals, include a protein source after training when possible, and combine nutrition with consistent resistance training and sleep.
source: Curated nutrition guidance aligned with sports nutrition principles
safety: Avoid extreme protein changes if the user has kidney disease or clinician-set restrictions.

content: For fat loss, prioritize protein and fiber at meals, reduce sugary drinks and frequent fried snacks, and use portions that keep energy intake reasonable without skipping core meals.
source: Curated nutrition guidance aligned with balanced-diet principles
safety: Avoid crash dieting, especially with deficiencies, pregnancy, diabetes, or medical conditions.

content: For iron deficiency support, keep tea/coffee and calcium-rich foods away from iron-rich meals or iron tablets when possible, and add vitamin C sources such as lemon, amla, guava, or citrus.
source: Curated nutrition guidance for iron absorption timing
safety: Do not change iron supplement dose or timing without clinician guidance.
